page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-30 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c3d server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 30 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 15 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. It is configured with 59.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.97 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. Passmark memory scores also rank in the top 25%, and text generation using a small 135M LLM model achieves a strong rate of 314.72 tokens per second. Most other metrics, including multi-core CPU performance, memory read bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations, fall within the average range. However, memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 25.11 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-highcpu-30 offers a high density of compute resources relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound tasks where paying for excess RAM is unnecessary. The primary tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage and the low memory-per-core ratio, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases. This instance is best suited for workloads such as text processing, web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-30 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 30 dedicated vCPUs across 15 physical cores, 59.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, text processing, and small-model LLM text generation, all ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is a notable bottleneck, placing in the bottom 25%. With a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.97 GB per vCPU, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for CPU-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory allocations. It is suited for text processing, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-30 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 30 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 59 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but lacks local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-30 features 30 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 59 GB RAM, and strong single-core CPU performance for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, optimized for CPU-bound workloads requiring minimal memory overhead.

59.0 GB of system memory providing 1.97 GB per vCPU
